channel.title: GitHub Copilot Bulletin — English / Biweekly / Decision makers
channel.description: Latest GitHub Copilot updates for Decision makers (Biweekly, English)

item.title: Copilot Bulletin Biweekly #3 — April 1 to April 15, 2026 · Decision-Makers Edition
item.description: GitHub Copilot is consolidating its enterprise credentials this fortnight. Two pivotal thresholds were crossed: data sovereignty for regulated industries, and precision measurement of AI agent adoption across organizations. Paired with expanded model choice for third-party coding agents, these advances signal that Copilot is no longer a productivity experiment—it is infrastructure-grade tooling for organizations that cannot afford ambiguity.

item.title: Copilot Bulletin Biweekly #2 — March 23 to April 6, 2026 · Decision-Makers Edition
item.description: Over the past two weeks, GitHub has systematically matured Copilot cloud agent from a pull request tool into an enterprise-ready development platform. Expanded autonomous capabilities — research, planning, and code iteration — combined with a wave of organization-level governance controls signal that GitHub is ready for organizations to deploy AI agents at scale.

item.title: Copilot Bulletin Biweekly #1 — March 2 to March 16, 2026 · Decision-Makers Edition
item.description: Over the past two weeks, GitHub Copilot has crossed a clear threshold: from developer productivity tool to enterprise platform. With 60 million code reviews completed, a new SDK for embedding agentic execution into applications, and enterprise-grade security architecture for agentic workflows, the signal is that Copilot is becoming foundational infrastructure — not just an assistant.
